revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=健田須賀神社| 所在地=茨城県結城市結城195| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=武渟川別命
須佐之男命| 社格=式内社（小）・県社| 創建=不詳（旧健田神社）
[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）（旧須賀神社）| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[11月15日]]| 神事=| 画像=}} '''健田須賀神社'''（たけだすがじんじゃ）は、[[茨城県]][[結城市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。明治時代に健田神社と須賀神社を合祀したもので、このうち健田神社は[[式内社]]である。 [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]]（旧健田神社祭神）と[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]（旧須賀神社祭神）を祀る。 ==歴史== 健田神社は、創建の由緒は不詳であるが、結城国造の竹田臣が祖神の武渟川別命を祀ったものと伝えられる。武渟川別命は[[四道将軍]]の一人として東国を平定・開拓した人物である。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[下総国]][[結城郡]] 健田神社」として下総国11社のうちの1社として記載され、小社に列している。[[宝暦]]14年（[[1764年]]）、結城本郷小塙村の[[乗国寺]]の境内に遷座した。 須賀神社は、当地に封じられた[[結城氏]]が、[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）6月に[[尾張国]][[津島神社]]より勧請を受けて「天王社」として創建したものである。[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）、[[結城直朝]]が結城七社を定め、当社を結城郷の総社とした。明治元年、[[神仏分離]]令により天王社から須賀神社に改称した。 明治3年、健田神社は乗国寺から別れ、須賀神社に合祀されて健田須賀神社と称した。明治6年に郷社に列格し、昭和5年に県社に昇格した。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/shimousa/bun/smf200502-01.html 健田須賀神社]（延喜式神社の調査） {{DEFAULTSORT:たけたすかしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:結城市]]

{{神社| 名称=健田須賀神社| 所在地=茨城県結城市結城195| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=武渟川別命
須佐之男命| 社格=式内社（小）・県社| 創建=不詳（旧健田神社）
[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）（旧須賀神社）| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[11月15日]]| 神事=| 画像=}} '''健田須賀神社'''（たけだすがじんじゃ）は、[[茨城県]][[結城市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。明治時代に健田神社と須賀神社を合祀したもので、このうち健田神社は[[式内社]]である。 [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]]（旧健田神社祭神）と[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]（旧須賀神社祭神）を祀る。 ==歴史== 健田神社は、創建の由緒は不詳であるが、結城国造の竹田臣が祖神の武渟川別命を祀ったものと伝えられる。武渟川別命は[[四道将軍]]の一人として東国を平定・開拓した人物である。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[下総国]][[結城郡]] 健田神社」として下総国11社のうちの1社として記載され、小社に列している。[[宝暦]]14年（[[1764年]]）、結城本郷小塙村の[[乗国寺]]の境内に遷座した。 須賀神社は、当地に封じられた[[結城氏]]が、[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）6月に[[尾張国]][[津島神社]]より勧請を受けて「天王社」として創建したものである。[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）、[[結城直朝]]が結城七社を定め、当社を結城郷の総社とした。明治元年、[[神仏分離]]令により天王社から須賀神社に改称した。 明治3年、健田神社は乗国寺から別れ、須賀神社に合祀されて健田須賀神社と称した。明治6年に郷社に列格し、昭和5年に県社に昇格した。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/shimousa/bun/smf200502-01.html 健田須賀神社]（延喜式神社の調査） {{DEFAULTSORT:たけたすかしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧県社]] [[Category:結城市]]

{{神社| 名称=健田須賀神社| 所在地=茨城県結城市結城195| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=武渟川別命

{{神社 |名称 = 健田須賀神社 |画像 = [[File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = 茨城県結城市結城195 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = 武渟川別命

旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不詳（旧健田神社）
[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）（旧須賀神社） |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = [[天王社]] |札所等 = |例祭 =[[11月15日]] |神事 = |地図={{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=健田須賀神社|caption=健田須賀神社所在地|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''健田須賀神社'''（たけだすがじんじゃ）は、[[茨城県]][[結城市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。明治時代に'''健田神社'''と'''須賀神社'''を合祀したもので、このうち健田神社は[[式内社]]である。 [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]]（旧'''健田神社'''祭神）と[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]（旧'''須賀神社'''祭神）を祀る。 == 歴史 == '''健田神社'''は、創建の由緒は不詳であるが、[[結城国造]]の[[竹田臣]]が祖神の武渟川別命を祀ったものと伝えられる。武渟川別命は[[四道将軍]]の一人として東国を平定・開拓した人物である。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[下総国]][[結城郡]] 健田神社」として下総国11社のうちの1社として記載され、小社に列している。[[宝暦]]14年（[[1764年]]）、結城本郷[[小塙村]]にある結城家の菩提所である[[乗国寺|見龍山覺心院乘國寺]]（[[曹洞宗]]）の境内に遷座した。 '''須賀神社'''は、当地に封じられた[[結城氏]]が、[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）6月に[[尾張国]][[津島神社]]より勧請を受けて「'''天王社'''」として創建したものである。[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）、[[結城直朝]]が'''結城七社'''を定め、当社を[[結城郷]]の[[総社]]とした。明治元年、[[神仏分離]]令により天王社から須賀神社に改称した。 [[明治3年]]、健田神社は乗国寺から別れ、須賀神社に合祀されて'''健田須賀神社'''と称した。[[明治6年]]に郷社に列格し、[[昭和5年]]に県社に昇格した。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html 健田須賀神社] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html 乗国寺公式] {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=茨城県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけたすかしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:結城郡]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:津島神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:結城氏]] [[Category:茨城県の旧県社]] [[Category:結城市の建築物]] [[Category:結城市の歴史]]

14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:健田須賀神社 up to rev 83975897
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 健田須賀神社 |画像 = [[File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = [[茨城県]][[結城市]]結城195 |位置 = {{Coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000_region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=健田須賀神社}} |祭神 = [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]]
[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]（津島[[牛頭天王]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不詳（旧健田神社）
[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）（旧須賀神社） |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = [[天王社]] |札所等 = |例祭 =[[11月15日]] |神事 = |地図={{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=健田須賀神社|caption=健田須賀神社所在地|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''健田須賀神社'''（たけだすがじんじゃ）は、[[茨城県]][[結城市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。明治時代に'''健田神社'''と'''須賀神社'''を合祀したもので、このうち健田神社は[[式内社]]である。 [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]]（旧'''健田神社'''祭神）と[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]（旧'''須賀神社'''祭神）を祀る。 == 歴史 == '''健田神社'''は、創建の由緒は不詳であるが、[[結城国造]]の[[竹田臣]]が祖神の武渟川別命を祀ったものと伝えられる。武渟川別命は[[四道将軍]]の一人として東国を平定・開拓した人物である。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[下総国]][[結城郡]] 健田神社」として下総国11社のうちの1社として記載され、小社に列している。[[宝暦]]14年（[[1764年]]）、結城本郷[[小塙村]]にある結城家の菩提所である[[乗国寺|見龍山覺心院乘國寺]]（[[曹洞宗]]）の境内に遷座した。 '''須賀神社'''は、当地に封じられた[[結城氏]]が、[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）6月に[[尾張国]][[津島神社]]より勧請を受けて「'''天王社'''」として創建したものである。[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）、[[結城直朝]]が'''結城七社'''を定め、当社を[[結城郷]]の[[総社]]とした。明治元年、[[神仏分離]]令により天王社から須賀神社に改称した。 [[明治3年]]、健田神社は乗国寺から別れ、須賀神社に合祀されて'''健田須賀神社'''と称した。[[明治6年]]に郷社に列格し、[[昭和5年]]に県社に昇格した。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html 健田須賀神社] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html 乗国寺公式] {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=茨城県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけたすかしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:結城郡]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:津島神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:結城氏]] [[Category:茨城県の旧県社]] [[Category:結城市の建築物]] [[Category:結城市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 健田須賀神社 |画像 = [[File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = [[茨城県]][[結城市]]結城195 |位置 = {{Coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000_region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=健田須賀神社}} |祭神 = [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]]
[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]（津島[[牛頭天王]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不詳（旧健田神社）
[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）（旧須賀神社） |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = [[天王社]] |札所等 = |例祭 =[[11月15日]] |神事 = |地図={{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=健田須賀神社|caption=健田須賀神社所在地|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''健田須賀神社'''（たけだすがじんじゃ）は、[[茨城県]][[結城市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。明治時代に'''健田神社'''と'''須賀神社'''を合祀したもので、このうち健田神社は[[式内社]]である。 [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]]（旧'''健田神社'''祭神）と[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]（旧'''須賀神社'''祭神）を祀る。 == 歴史 == '''健田神社'''は、創建の由緒は不詳であるが、[[結城国造]]の[[竹田臣]]が祖神の武渟川別命を祀ったものと伝えられる。武渟川別命は[[四道将軍]]の一人として東国を平定・開拓した人物である。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[下総国]][[結城郡]] 健田神社」として下総国11社のうちの1社として記載され、小社に列している。[[宝暦]]14年（[[1764年]]）、結城本郷[[小塙村]]にある結城家の菩提所である[[乗国寺|見龍山覺心院乘國寺]]（[[曹洞宗]]）の境内に遷座した。 '''須賀神社'''は、当地に封じられた[[結城氏]]が、[[仁治]]3年（[[1242年]]）6月に[[尾張国]][[津島神社]]より勧請を受けて「'''天王社'''」として創建したものである。[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）、[[結城直朝]]が'''結城七社'''を定め、当社を[[結城郷]]の[[総社]]とした。明治元年、[[神仏分離]]令により天王社から須賀神社に改称した。 [[明治3年]]、健田神社は乗国寺から別れ、須賀神社に合祀されて'''健田須賀神社'''と称した。[[明治6年]]に郷社に列格し、[[昭和5年]]に県社に昇格した。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html 健田須賀神社] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html 乗国寺公式] {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=茨城県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけたすかしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:結城郡]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:津島神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:結城氏]] [[Category:茨城県の旧県社]] [[Category:結城市の建築物]] [[Category:結城市の歴史]]

[[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11387225]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11387225]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Name = Takeda Suga Shrine |image = File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki City]] Yūki 195 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.302222|N|139.874722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}}
[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Tsushima [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown (former Takeda Shrine)
1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = [[:en:November 15|November 15]] |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|qid=Q847569}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki City]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]]. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]] invited the deity from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|qq=|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki, Ibaraki]], Ibaraki Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000_region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takeda Suga Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Tsushima Gozu Tennō) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former Prefectural Shrine | established = Unknown (original Takeda Shrine)
1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki, Ibaraki]], Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]], who brought its deity from the [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimousa Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}}

[[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11387225]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11387225]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Name = Takeda Suga Shrine |image = File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki City]] Yūki 195 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.302222|N|139.874722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}}
[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Tsushima [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown (former Takeda Shrine)
1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = [[:en:November 15|November 15]] |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|WD=Q847569}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki City]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]]. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]] invited the deity from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|qq=|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * 1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki, Ibaraki]], Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]], who brought its deity from the [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimousa Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}}

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown (former Takeda Shrine)
1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = [[:en:November 15|November 15]] |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|WD=Q847569}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki City]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]]. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]] invited the deity from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|qq=|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * 1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki, Ibaraki]], Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]], who brought its deity from the [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimousa Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}}

1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = [[:en:November 15|November 15]] |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|WD=Q847569}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki City]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]]. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]] invited the deity from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * 1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki, Ibaraki]], Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]], who brought its deity from the [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimousa Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}}

In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takeda Suga Shrine (健田須賀神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG|300px]] | location = [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki, Ibaraki]], Ibaraki Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000_region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takeda Suga Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Tsushima Gozu Tennō) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former Prefectural Shrine | established = Unknown (original Takeda Shrine)
1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki, Ibaraki]], Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]], who brought its deity from the [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimousa Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}}

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown (former Takeda Shrine)
1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = [[:en:November 15|November 15]] |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|WD=Q847569}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki City]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]]. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]] invited the deity from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takeda Suga Shrine (健田須賀神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG|300px]] | location = [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki, Ibaraki]], Ibaraki Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000_region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takeda Suga Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Tsushima Gozu Tennō) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former Prefectural Shrine | established = Unknown (original Takeda Shrine)
1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki, Ibaraki]], Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]], who brought its deity from the [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimousa Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}}

[[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11387225]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11387225]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Name = Takeda Suga Shrine |image = File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki City]] Yūki 195 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.302222|N|139.874722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}}
[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Tsushima [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown (former Takeda Shrine)
1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = [[:en:November 15|November 15]] |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|WD=Q847569}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yūki, Ibaraki|Yūki City]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]]. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]] invited the deity from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]]. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a [[:en:Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the [[:en:Yūki clan|Yūki clan]], who brought its deity from the [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimousa Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
