[[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Name = Takeda Suga Shrine |image = File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki City|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}} Yūki 195 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.302222|N|139.874722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Tsushima {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown (former Takeda Shrine)
1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shimmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = {{ill|November 15|en|November 15|WD=Q2982|lt=November 15|1=November 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|WD=Q847569|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki City|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}} invited the deity from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Ibaraki Prefecture, Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Tsushima Gozu Tennō) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former Prefectural Shrine | established = Unknown (original Takeda Shrine)
1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimousa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}, who brought its deity from the {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}} [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimousa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Name = Takeda Suga Shrine |image = File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki City|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}} Yūki 195 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.302222|N|139.874722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Tsushima {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown (former Takeda Shrine)
1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shimmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = {{ill|November 15|en|November 15|WD=Q2982|lt=November 15|1=November 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|WD=Q847569|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki City|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}} invited the deity from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Takeda Suga Shrine Official Website] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Jōkoku-ji Temple Official Website] {{Ibaraki Shrines List}} {{Sōja_shrines}} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takeda Suga Shrine (健田須賀神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Ibaraki Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000_region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takeda Suga Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Tsushima Gozu Tennō) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former Prefectural Shrine | established = Unknown (original Takeda Shrine)
1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimousa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}, who brought its deity from the {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}} [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimousa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Name = Takeda Suga Shrine |image = File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki City|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}} Yūki 195 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.302222|N|139.874722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Tsushima {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown (former Takeda Shrine)
1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shimmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = {{ill|November 15|en|November 15|WD=Q2982|lt=November 15|1=November 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|WD=Q847569|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki City|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}} invited the deity from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * (健田須賀神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Ibaraki Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000_region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takeda Suga Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Tsushima Gozu Tennō) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former Prefectural Shrine | established = Unknown (original Takeda Shrine)
1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimousa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}, who brought its deity from the {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}} [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Name = Takeda Suga Shrine |image = File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki City|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}} Yūki 195 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.302222|N|139.874722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Tsushima {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown (former Takeda Shrine)
1242 (former Suga Shrine) |Main_Building = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shimmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other_Names = {{ill|Tenno-sha|ja|天王社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} |Festivals = {{ill|November 15|en|November 15|WD=Q2982|lt=November 15|1=November 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Takeda Suga Shrine|caption=Takeda Suga Shrine location|coordinates= {{coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}|prefecture = {{ill|Yuki|WD=Q847569|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社, ''Takeda Suga Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki City|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}} invited the deity from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Tsushima Gozu Tennō) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former Prefectural Shrine | established = Unknown (original Takeda Shrine)
1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimousa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}, who brought its deity from the {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}} [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine was historically Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|ja|武渟川別|lt=Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|qq=}} (the former deity of Takeda Shrine) and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (the former deity of Suga Shrine). It is a provincial soja with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == '''Takeda Shrine''' has an but it is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage of the {{ill|Takeda no Omi|ja|竹田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestor, Takenuokawa Wake no Mikoto. This deity was one of the {{ill|Shidō Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, a group of four generals dispatched to pacify and develop the eastern provinces. The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Takeda Shrine of Yūki District, Shimōsa Province," making it one of the 11 shrines listed for Shimōsa Province. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}} invited the deity from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Tsushima Gozu Tennō) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former Prefectural Shrine | established = Unknown (original Takeda Shrine)
1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimousa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}, who brought its deity from the {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}} [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

It was formed during the Meiji period through the merger of '''Takeda Shrine''', a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple that serves as the ancestral temple of the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}. '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) when the ruling {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}} invited the deity from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, naming it '''Tenno-sha''' (天王社). In 1343 (Kōei 2), {{ill|Yūki Naoasa|ja|結城直朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki Naoasa|1=Yūki Naotomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606616}} designated it as one of the '''Yūki Seven Shrines''' (結城七社) and made it the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} (main provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yūki District|ja|結城郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms in 1868 (Meiji 1), the shrine was renamed '''Suga Shrine'''. In 1870 (Meiji 3), Takeda Shrine was separated from Jōkoku-ji and merged with Suga Shrine, forming '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1930 (Shōwa 5), it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Takeda Suga Shrine Official Website] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Jōkoku-ji Temple Official Website] {{Ibaraki Shrines List}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} }} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Sōja_shrines}} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takeda Suga Shrine (健田須賀神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takedasuga Jinja.JPG|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Ibaraki Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|08|N|139|52|29|E|scale:30000_region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takeda Suga Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Tsushima Gozu Tennō) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former Prefectural Shrine | established = Unknown (original Takeda Shrine)
1242 (original Suga Shrine) | architecture = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | alternate_names = Tennōsha | festivals = November 15 }} '''Takeda Suga Shrine''' (健田須賀神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yūki, Ibaraki|en|Yūki, Ibaraki|WD=Q847569|lt=Yūki, Ibaraki|1=Yūki, Ibaraki}}, Japan. The shrine is classified as a former Prefectural Shrine and was formed in the Meiji period by merging the {{ill|Takeda Shrine|ja|健田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 健田須賀神社}} and {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, with the former being a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}, the deity of the original Takeda Shrine, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the deity of the original Suga Shrine. == History == The '''Takeda Shrine''' has an unclear founding date but is said to have been established by the Yūki Province {{ill|Yūki clan|ja|結城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. Takenouchi no Sukune was one of the {{ill|Shido Shōgun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, credited with pacifying and developing eastern Japan. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Kenda Shrine" under the 11 shrines of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimousa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}. In 1764 (Hōreki 14), the shrine was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Jōkoku-ji|ja|乗国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple. The '''Suga Shrine''' was established in 1242 (Ninji 3) by the {{ill|Yūki clan|en|Yūki clan|WD=Q2975399|lt=Yūki clan|1=Yūki clan}}, who brought its deity from the {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province. Initially called "Tennōsha," it became the collective shrine for Yūki district in 1343 (Kōei 2) under the Yūki clan. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the name was changed from Tennōsha to Suga Shrine. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the Takeda Shrine separated from Jōkoku-ji and was merged with the Suga Shrine to form the '''Takeda Suga Shrine'''. It was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 (Meiji 6) and elevated to a Prefectural Shrine in 1930 (Shōwa 5). == External Links == * [http://www.takedasugajinja.com/index.html Official website of Takeda Suga Shrine] * [http://joukokuji.web.fc2.com/cheng_guo_si/you_xu.html Official website of Jōkoku-ji] {{Shinto topics}} {{Ibaraki shrines}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|健田須賀神社|version=83975897|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:健田須賀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11387225}} [[Category:Architecture of Yūki City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yuki]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuki District]] [[Category:Yūki clan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
